title: Number-line generator (integer/fraction/decimal/blank; ranges + steps)
context: Number lines are used across all years of elementary mathematics: counting, skip-counting, addition, subtraction, fractions, decimals, and negative numbers. The pedagogical requirement is exact interval equality -- an unequal interval implies a false numerical relationship. The generator must support configurable ranges and step sizes for integers, fractions, decimals, and blank (open) lines, and the harness must verify interval equality geometrically.
objective: Implement a number-line generator module that produces integer, fraction, decimal, and blank number lines with configurable range and step, with geometrically verified equal intervals.
acceptanceCriteria: Supports four line types: integer, fraction (with configurable denominator), decimal, and blank/open (no labels, just tick marks or no marks).
Range and step are configurable via the spec; origin and direction are always correct.
Intervals are provably equal: the verification harness confirms interval equality geometrically (not just by checking the spec value), and a math-educator review sign-off is recorded.
Labels are legible at standard and large-print sizes in grayscale.
Generates all standard variants: A4/Letter x colour/grayscale/ink-saver x standard/large-print.
MIT-licensed; all commits DCO-signed; passes pnpm build, pnpm test, and CI verification gate.
resources: tasks/math-manip-code-002.json -- verification harness (must extend to check interval equality)
output: A number-line generator module integrated into the generator engine package, with spec files for integer/fraction/decimal/blank variants and example output PDFs, in a pull request, MIT-licensed.
